claims.text: The proposed MARL framework is validated in the Vissim traffic simulator.
claims.source_quote: This paper proposes a robust Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) framework validated in the Vissim traffic simulator.

claims.text: The framework integrates a training strategy called Turning Ratio Randomization that exposes agents to dynamic turning probabilities.
claims.source_quote: Turning Ratio Randomization, a training strategy that exposes agents to dynamic turning probabilities to enhance robustness against unseen scenarios

claims.text: Turning Ratio Randomization enhances robustness against unseen scenarios.
claims.source_quote: Turning Ratio Randomization, a training strategy that exposes agents to dynamic turning probabilities to enhance robustness against unseen scenarios

claims.text: The framework uses an Exponential Phase Duration Adjustment action space that performs cyclical, exponential phase adjustments.
claims.source_quote: a stability-oriented Exponential Phase Duration Adjustment action space, which balances responsiveness and precision through cyclical, exponential phase adjustments

claims.text: The framework uses a Neighbor-Based Observation scheme utilizing the MAPPO algorithm.
claims.source_quote: a Neighbor-Based Observation scheme utilizing the MAPPO algorithm with Centralized Training with Decentralized Execution (CTDE)

claims.text: The framework employs Centralized Training with Decentralized Execution (CTDE).
claims.source_quote: a Neighbor-Based Observation scheme utilizing the MAPPO algorithm with Centralized Training with Decentralized Execution (CTDE)

claims.text: The neighbor-based observation approach with centralized updates approximates the efficacy of global observations while maintaining scalable local communication.
claims.source_quote: By leveraging centralized updates, this approach approximates the efficacy of global observations while maintaining scalable local communication.

claims.text: The proposed framework reduces average waiting time by over 10% compared to standard RL baselines.
claims.source_quote: Experimental results demonstrate that our framework outperforms standard RL baselines, reducing average waiting time by over 10%.

claims.text: The proposed framework outperforms standard RL baselines.
claims.source_quote: Experimental results demonstrate that our framework outperforms standard RL baselines, reducing average waiting time by over 10%.

claims.text: The proposed model exhibits superior generalization in unseen traffic scenarios compared to standard RL baselines.
claims.source_quote: The proposed model exhibits superior generalization in unseen traffic scenarios and maintains high control stability

claims.text: The proposed model maintains high control stability.
claims.source_quote: The proposed model exhibits superior generalization in unseen traffic scenarios and maintains high control stability

claims.text: Existing RL approaches for traffic signal control often overfit static patterns.
claims.source_quote: Existing approaches often overfit static patterns and use action spaces incompatible with driver expectations.

claims.text: Existing RL approaches for traffic signal control use action spaces incompatible with driver expectations.
claims.source_quote: Existing approaches often overfit static patterns and use action spaces incompatible with driver expectations.

claims.text: RL in Traffic Signal Control faces limited generalization to dynamic traffic flow variations in real-world deployment.
claims.source_quote: Reinforcement Learning (RL) in Traffic Signal Control (TSC) faces significant hurdles in real-world deployment due to limited generalization to dynamic traffic flow variations.
